Absent expedited processing, you can expect a substantive response within 20 business days of receipt, subject to any extension permitted under the statute. If we anticipate needing additional time or if the scope requires narrowing to meet the timeline, we will contact you directly.

Please direct any questions or clarifications to [FOIA officer contact].

Sincerely,
[FOIA Officer]
[Agency]

(2) If we have this data clean and ready, releasing it promptly and in a usable format is the right posture for an agency whose credibility depends on being seen as rigorous. Don't slow-walk it.

Take 4:30. Don't move Hiro — rescheduling internal one-on-ones to accommodate a board member's last-minute ask sends the wrong signal about whose time gets protected, and Hiro's meetings are where the work actually gets done. 4:30 also gives you the afternoon to prepare, which you'll want: this is almost certainly the settlement conversation, and you should walk in with the expected-value and precedent analysis in hand rather than improvising.

One more thought: ask Meera whether she should join. If the conversation stays at the level of litigation posture, you're fine solo. If Alvarez is going to raise institutional or political considerations, having counsel in the room — and in the record — is protective for both of you.

Yes — confirm external posting alongside internal-first, that's our standard and I want it on record. Worth adding one line acknowledging last quarter's exception was exactly that — an exception — and thanking her for keeping us accountable to our own practice.

TECHNICAL SUMMARY — ARGUS SYSTEMS ENFORCEMENT MATTER
Prepared for Board Packet, [Date]
Author: Dana [Last Name], [Title]
Standard Format — Board Confidential

1. Evidentiary Basis and Findings

The enforcement action against Argus Systems concerns an algorithmic hiring tool deployed across six regulated industries between 2019 and 2023. The central evidentiary finding is a documented disparate impact: qualified candidates from three protected groups were screened out at rates 40–60% lower than comparable candidates from unprotected groups. The disparity is supported by the agency's statistical analysis of Argus's own output data, obtained through compulsory process, and has been reviewed by the case team's technical staff. Argus's rebuttal characterizes the disparity as a statistical artifact absent discriminatory intent and points to remediation completed in 2023; the agency's position is that disparate impact of this magnitude is actionable under the applicable standard regardless of intent, and that post-hoc remediation does not resolve liability for the deployment period.

2. Status of Contested Evidence

Three internal Argus communications, obtained via subpoena, indicate that engineering staff were aware of differential model outputs in 2021 and did not escalate the issue through internal compliance channels. Argus has asserted attorney-client privilege over these communications; a ruling on the privilege claim is expected in approximately six weeks. The agency's litigation assessment does not depend on admission of these communications — the disparate-impact case is independently sufficient on the statistical record — but their admission would materially strengthen the record on the question of knowledge and corporate response. No other evidentiary admissibility issues are currently pending.

3. Current Posture

Outside counsel's final litigation assessment places the probability of prevailing at trial at approximately 85%, with the case team unanimous in recommending litigation on the merits. Argus has proposed a settlement of $4.2 million with no admission of wrongdoing; this figure falls below the median expected value of the case under the current assessment, and a no-admission resolution would not establish precedent on the underlying standard. The board is asked to weigh, in addition to expected monetary value, the precedential effect of the chosen resolution on the agency's enforcement posture across the covered industries in which comparable tools remain in deployment.

— Dana [Last Name], [Title]
